title: Benchmark Mir Simulator Configuration Schema
description: JSON Schema for the benchmarkMirSimulator configuration file (e.g., config_random.yaml).

setup.description: General setup configuration for the simulator.

mode.description: Generation mode for the simulator. Use 'random' for procedural generation, 'manual' to specify topology nodes and edges manually.

infeasible_penalty.description: Penalty cost returned by the solver when a placement is infeasible.

greedy_solver.description: Configuration parameters for the Greedy solver module (can be empty {}).

ilp_solver.description: Configuration for the ILP solver module.

timeLimit.description: Time limit in seconds for the solver execution.

gapRel.description: Relative gap tolerance for the optimal solution (e.g., 0.05 for 5%).

objective.description: Objective function type or algorithm for the solver.

weights.description: Weights assigned to different optimization metrics when using multi-objective.

server_usage.description: Weight applied to server resource usage.

trigger_policy.description: Defines when the ILP solver should be triggered.

interval_seconds.description: Time interval in seconds for solve_every_t_seconds or combined.

batch_size.description: Number of events to accumulate before solving in solve_every_n_events.

critical_events.description: List of action names that forcefully trigger the solver in solve_on_event_types or combined.

topology.description: Used only when setup.mode is 'manual'. Describes the exact nodes and edges in the graph.

app.description: Configuration for application generation and demand.

architecture.description: Software architecture type. 'microservice' models Service Function Chains.

topology_model.description: Topology generation model. 'sfc' for linear chain, 'directed_scale_free' for DAG.

scale_free_settings.description: Parameters for the directed_scale_free generation model.

num_services_range.description: Minimum and maximum number of microservices per application graph.

profiles.description: Profiles defining CPU/RAM distributions for microservices.

prob.description: Probability of this profile being selected.

attributes.description: Dynamic attributes for microservices (e.g. cpu, ram) mapped to their distributions.

network.description: Dynamic network requirements for the app.

distribution.description: Statistical distribution from which the application popularity is drawn.

model.description: Popularity distribution model, typically 'zipf'. Deprecated: use distribution instead.

alpha.description: Alpha parameter for Zipf's law. Deprecated: use distribution instead.

local_app_ratio.description: Ratio of apps that are bound to a specific geographical region.

local_radius_influence.description: Radius of influence around a local app where popularity is boosted.

num_new_users.description: Distribution to sample the initial amount of users per application generated.

actions.description: Periodic actions targeting applications (e.g., removing or updating apps).

user.description: Configuration for the generation, mobility, and behavior of simulation users.

num_users.description: Total number of initial users to spawn.

spatial_distribution.description: Model assigning spatial coordinates to spawned users.

model.description: E.g., 'thomas_cluster' spawns users near hotspots, 'random_uniform' spawns them randomly across the plane.

spread.description: Standard deviation around the hotspot for user clustering.

spatial_region.description: Boundary constraints for user movement and node layout scaling.

width.description: Maximum width (X-axis limit) for the spatial region.

height.description: Maximum height (Y-axis limit) for the spatial region.

num_vertical_streets.description: Number of vertical streets uniformly distributed.

num_horizontal_streets.description: Number of horizontal streets uniformly distributed.

mobility.description: Mobility model governing how users move through the environment.

model.description: E.g., 'manhattan' constrains movement to an X/Y grid, 'random_direction' allows moving in any angle.

turn_probabilities.description: Probabilities of turning at an intersection for manhattan model.

speed.description: Base traversal speed per movement event or a distribution

coverage_radius.description: Maximum Euclidean distance the user can drift before losing signal or a distribution

request_ratio.description: Distribution defining the frequency/amount of service requests issued by the user.

actions.description: Periodic actions applied to users (e.g., move_user, remove_user).

global_spawner.description: Actions generating entirely new entities dynamically during the simulation.

new_user.description: Action responsible for spawning new users over time.

new_app.description: Action responsible for spawning new applications over time.

actions.description: Periodic actions applied to the graph elements (e.g. node/edge failures).

attributes.description: Node specific attributes like ram, cpu, storage, and layer classification.
Edge specific attributes like delay and bandwidth.

num_nodes.description: Total number of nodes to generate in the infrastructure graph.

model.description: Configuration for the chosen infrastructure model.

name.description: Algorithm used to generate the infrastructure graph topology.

distributionDef.description: Statistical distribution specification or layered mapping.

type.description: The type of statistical distribution to draw values from.

a.description: Shape parameter for Pareto, Beta, Weibull, or Zipf.

scale.description: Scale parameter for Exponential or Weibull.

mean.description: Mean value for Normal or Lognormal.

min.description: Minimum clamped limit applied to the generated value.

max.description: Maximum clamped limit applied to the generated value.

actionItem.description: Configuration object describing a stochastic event, sub-action, or macro-event.

type_object.description: The entity type this action originates from or affects.

action_type.description: The name of the action to execute (e.g., 'disable_node', 'surge_popularity').

frequency.description: Statistical distribution specifying the time interval until the next event trigger.

impact.description: Contextual parameters for macro-events, including the composed_of array.

composed_of.description: List of sub-actions to execute when this encapsulated macro-event triggers.

impact_params.description: Parameters for individual sub-actions, such as target_resolution.

attributeDef.description: Definition structure describing how an element's attribute is assigned.

mode.description: Mechanism determining how values are generated across nodes/edges. homogenic implies the same base distribution. centrality_based alters values by betweenness.

distribution.description: Base distribution or layered mapping from which attribute values are drawn.

min.description: Minimum clamped limit applied to the generated value.

max.description: Maximum clamped limit applied to the generated value.

centrality_type.description: Whether high centrality results in a higher multiplier (direct) or lower multiplier (inverted).

thresholds.description: Thresholds used by modes like centrality_based_layer.
